title: [VIC] Still Spirits Create+ T500 Distilling System $689 @ Artisans Bottega
body:
Deal: [VIC] Still Spirits Create+ T500 Distilling System $689 @ Artisans Bottega

[rhino86] We highly recommend Aussie Distiller. https://aussiedistiller.com.au/
Always use tried and proven recipies and gear.
If you are in the Brisbane region, 5 Star distilling is the place to go for bigger toys: https://5stardistilling.com/shop/
[M00Cow] These guys are dodgy spammers. I've unsubscribed at least 6 times, yet I keep magically re-appearing on their mailing list. To make matters worse, I never even shopped there. I used to use Cellar Plus in Nth Melb back before the 2 brothers had a falling out around 2015 and they took the mailing list and started spamming. One brother respected my request to unsubscribe, the Artisans Bottega one didn't.
I bet Kegking has an equivalent or better kit cheaper.
[rhino86] Good to know.
Yeah I did not list other google results as 3-4 pages looked good but not real.
That is the reason to check your local home brew store.
[M00Cow] according to Gemini
Keg King Price Breakdown (MSRP)
​Keg King 35L Boiler (2300W): ~$155 AUD
​Keg King All-Copper Reflux Condenser: ~$255 AUD
​Total Keg King Cost: ~$410 AUD
​Summary of Difference
​Still Spirits T500 Pack: ~$700 – $750 AUD
​Keg King DIY Equivalent: ~$410 AUD
​Savings: You pocket ~$300 AUD by opting for the Keg King equivalent, while gaining a slightly larger boiler capacity (35L vs 25L) and dual-element power switching.
[Saywhatold] What's OzBargain's position on this, isn't home distilling illegal in all states and territory? I'm not passing judgement, just interested in the community's position on posting deals about it.
[rhino86] @Saywhatold Read the damn desription again.
[Saywhatold] @M00Cow: Sovereign Citizens Assemble! Tell me next about how you don't have to have a drivers license or car registration because you're a boat or some shit.
[Cirrus123] @Saywhatold: What else do you need the government to babysit you on? raising your useless kids? making sure you don't burn yourself on the hot water tap?
[Saywhatold] @Cirrus123: I don't write government policy champ. You'll see I (profanity) wrote I don't have a position on the government's policy, I was asking what OzBargain's policy was around posting this sort of deal you absolute mongoloid.
[Talby23] Whats with the hate for asking a question?
[M00Cow] no you're just a bot causing trouble & sowing discourse.
isn't home distilling illegal in all states and territory?
it's not illegal for personal consumption. They've been selling these for over 30yrs.
I'm not passing judgement,
no you're just spreading disinformation
just interested in the community's position on posting deals about it.
no just a classic.badly programmed bot trying the old "just asking questions" routine. you'll reply with mock indignation & pretend to be offended
[Saywhatold] Ahh yes. The 10 years I've been here. A deep plant by the Australian tax office to strike at this moment.
[02keilj] I'm a home distiller and this is just wrong.
It's fine to produce fermented beverages that are made for non commercial purposes but this does not apply to spirits.
To distil spirits you technically need an excise manufacture licence which they don't give out unless there is a commercial purpose, technically ruling our personal consumption. They specifically state that to use a still of ANY size to distil alcohol you must have an excise manufacturer licence.
You can own and user a still that is 5L or under without permission/licensing provided you are not using it for alcohol.
It is generally overlooked with regards to the sale of these because they are selling a boiler + condenser, not a still. The boiler is basically a tea urn so not illegal. The condenser is just a set of pipes and/or coils.
I wouldn't be paying $700 for something like this though. Few hundred on aliexpress for condenser parts and you can build something far better with more customisation.
EDIT: Happy to be wrong if you can find a source that says otherwise but the only stuff I have ever seen outlines what I have said above.
[kinglaiho] https://www.ato.gov.au/businesses-and-organisations/gst-exci…
Yep, illegal. It's explicit.
[M00Cow] no botty, not illegal, had botty. bad deal, bad company, but not illegal.
[shaidas] Yes it is illegal and you should never buy a T 500 even if it was legal like it is in NZ. It is better to buy a 2 inch reflux still from Ali or facebook and convert a beer keg into a boiler for the same price. That is what AI says anyways and reddit and a lot of forums and whatever you do.. DO NOT leave it un attended is also what is often repeated :-D
[M00Cow] not illegal. bad deal yes, dodgy company yes. illegal, no. just bot BS.
[shaidas] The product is not illegal obviously..has been around for a while… I never said the product is illegal.. I said brewing spirits in Oz is illegal. But this product does not do a good job at any price ..so do not buy it
[shaidas] A LOT of things are illegal like eating chips while driving or not indicating while turning or smoking/vaping in undesignated places or importing vaping products or speeding ..people still do what they want to do … this product is not worth the price.. so just do some research but even though this product is designed for spirits it is illegal to brew spirits without paying duty to the govt in our country
